message:
feat(common,beads): add process-triage schema tests, update issue tracking, and add bd-vvmd.2.3 bead

Extend process_triage.rs with schema-level contract tests that verify
the response schema exposes audit-record fields (policy_version,
decision_code, audit_required) and the full failure-kind taxonomy
(detector_uncertain, policy_violation, transport_error, etc.). Add
reusable schema introspection helpers (extract_ref_name,
find_schema_properties, definition_properties, definition_enum).

Minor whitespace fix in patterns.rs tests.

Update .beads/issues.jsonl with current epic state.
Add bd-vvmd.2.3 bead marker.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -392,7 +392,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.2","title":"Workstream: Cargo Path-Dependency Closure + Remote Preflight Verification","description":"Purpose: make remote compilation deterministic for projects using local Cargo path dependencies. Implement transitive dependency graph discovery, closure planning, multi-root transfer support, and pre-build remote verification. Outcome: commands that currently fail remotely due to missing local path crates succeed without user intervention, with safe fallback to local execution when convergence cannot be guaranteed.

## Success Criteria
- Resolver/planner/transfer/verification pipeline covers transitive path-dependency closures.
- Remote execution is attempted only when required roots/files are provably present and fresh.
- Cache identity tracks dependency-closure changes to avoid stale artifact reuse.
- Policy and tests enforce precise fail-open semantics for unresolved/unsafe conditions.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:00.427065865Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T16:34:24.564502617Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cargo","path-deps","rch","rch-rel-2026q1","transfer"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:11.587626298Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.2.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:24:21.200818801Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.2.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:24:21.403949617Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":131,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Critical success metric: projects with local Cargo path dependencies should remote-build successfully whenever closure can be converged; otherwise RCH must fail-open with explicit reason codes and no misleading partial state. Resolver/planner/transfer/verification/hash/policy are split to keep correctness boundaries auditable.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:20:20Z"},{"id":142,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Design constraints: path-dependency closure must be complete, deterministic, and root-constrained. Resolver computes transitive local path graph; planner turns it into ordered sync/verify steps; transfer uses auditable manifests. If any dependency cannot be proven present/fresh on worker, behavior must remain fail-open to local execution with reason-coded telemetry. Success criterion: cross-repo Cargo projects build remotely without manual repo copying.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:27:58Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.2.1","title":"Implement Cargo local path-dependency graph resolver (workspace + transitive)","description":"Build resolver that inspects Cargo metadata/manifests to compute full transitive closure of local path dependencies, including workspace members and nested path deps. Must normalize paths via foundation utilities and report unresolved/unsafe references with explicit diagnostics.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Resolver discovers direct and transitive Cargo path dependencies across workspace boundaries.
- Resolution honors canonical path policy and rejects out-of-scope roots with explicit diagnostics.
- Output includes deterministic dependency graph ordering for stable planning/hashing.
- Error taxonomy distinguishes manifest parse failures, missing paths, and cyclic/path-policy violations.
- Unit tests cover workspace, virtual workspace, nested manifests, and malformed metadata scenarios.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:02.821293956Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T19:23:19.463817275Z","closed_at":"2026-02-16T19:23:19.463798409Z","close_reason":"Implemented cargo path-dependency resolver with metadata+fallback traversal, canonical policy enforcement, deterministic graph ordering, and unit coverage; see latest artifact comment","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cargo","path-deps","rch-rel-2026q1","resolver"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:11.994905152Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":148,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Resolver implementation note: prefer Cargo metadata as primary source but support robust manifest fallback for failure cases. Normalize every discovered path through canonical root utilities before graph insertion; mark unresolved/out-of-root deps explicitly. Output should include machine-usable closure payload that downstream planner and hashing can consume without reparsing Cargo state.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:28:21Z"},{"id":211,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed `bd-vvmd.2.1` by implementing a shared Cargo local path-dependency resolver with canonical path policy enforcement, deterministic graph output, explicit error taxonomy, and focused unit coverage.

## Summary
Added new shared resolver module:
- `rch-common/src/cargo_path_deps.rs`

Exported resolver API via:
- `rch-common/src/lib.rs`

The resolver supports:
- `cargo metadata` as primary graph source,
- recursive manifest parsing fallback,
- canonical path normalization via topology policy utilities,
- deterministic package/edge ordering,
- explicit error categories for manifest/metadata/path-policy/cycle/missing-path failures.

## Acceptance Criteria Mapping

### 1) Discover direct + transitive Cargo path dependencies across workspace boundaries
Implemented graph resolver APIs:
- `resolve_cargo_path_dependency_graph` (`rch-common/src/cargo_path_deps.rs:201`)
- `resolve_cargo_path_dependency_graph_with_policy` (`rch-common/src/cargo_path_deps.rs:208`)
- metadata-first + fallback strategy (`rch-common/src/cargo_path_deps.rs:219`)

Graph model:
- `CargoPathDependencyGraph` (`rch-common/src/cargo_path_deps.rs:21`)
- `CargoPathDependencyPackage` / `CargoPathDependencyEdge`

Coverage:
- workspace transitive case (`resolves_workspace_transitive_path_dependencies`, `rch-common/src/cargo_path_deps.rs:1389`)
- virtual workspace + alias root case (`resolves_virtual_workspace_members_from_alias_path`, `rch-common/src/cargo_path_deps.rs:1456`)
- nested manifest transitive closure (`resolves_nested_manifest_transitive_closure`, `rch-common/src/cargo_path_deps.rs:1507`)

### 2) Canonical path policy + out-of-scope rejection diagnostics
All discovered paths flow through topology normalization:
- `normalize_path_for_policy` (`rch-common/src/cargo_path_deps.rs:563`)

Explicit absolute out-of-scope guard added prior to existence checks:
- `validate_absolute_dependency_scope` (`rch-common/src/cargo_path_deps.rs:532`)
- invoked for metadata deps (`rch-common/src/cargo_path_deps.rs:706`)
- invoked for manifest fallback deps (`rch-common/src/cargo_path_deps.rs:993`)

Coverage:
- out-of-root fixture expectation (`outside_root_reports_path_policy_violation`, `rch-common/src/cargo_path_deps.rs:1628`)

### 3) Deterministic dependency graph ordering
Determinism is enforced with ordered collections:
- `PartialGraph` uses `BTreeMap`/`BTreeSet` for nodes/adjacency (`rch-common/src/cargo_path_deps.rs:323`)
- `finalize_graph` emits sorted node/edge output (`rch-common/src/cargo_path_deps.rs:396`)

Coverage:
- deterministic sort assertion in workspace test (`rch-common/src/cargo_path_deps.rs:1426`)

### 4) Error taxonomy for parse/missing/cycle/policy failures
Error taxonomy implemented:
- `CargoPathDependencyErrorKind` with:
  - `ManifestParseFailure`
  - `MissingPathDependency`
  - `CyclicDependency`
  - `PathPolicyViolation`
  - `MetadataParseFailure`
  - `MetadataInvocationFailure`
  (`rch-common/src/cargo_path_deps.rs:60`)

### 5) Unit tests cover workspace, virtual workspace, nested manifests, malformed metadata
Unit tests added under module `#[cfg(test)]` (`rch-common/src/cargo_path_deps.rs:1243`) with explicit cases:
- workspace transitive
- virtual workspace + alias
- nested manifests
- malformed metadata fallback
- malformed manifest parse error
- missing path dependency
- outside canonical root violation
- cyclic dependency detection

## Integration Exports
Resolver APIs exported in `rch-common` public surface:
- module registration + public re-exports in `rch-common/src/lib.rs:12`, `rch-common/src/lib.rs:51`

## Validation Evidence
Executed:
1. `cargo fmt --check` -> PASS
2. `cargo test -p rch-common cargo_path_deps -- --nocapture` -> PASS (8 passed)
3. `tests/e2e/path_dependency_fixtures.sh` -> PASS
4. `cargo clippy -p rch-common --all-targets -- -D warnings` -> PASS
5. `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets` -> PASS (remote toolchain missing -> expected local fallback)
6. `rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` -> FAIL on pre-existing unrelated lint:
   - `rch/src/commands/daemon.rs:627` `clippy::items_after_test_module`

Conclusion: `bd-vvmd.2.1` acceptance criteria are satisfied with deterministic resolver behavior, explicit policy diagnostics, and comprehensive targeted unit coverage.
","created_at":"2026-02-16T19:23:16Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.2.2","title":"Build dependency-closure planner constrained to canonical repo roots","description":"Create planning layer that converts resolver output into ordered sync/verification actions. Constrain accepted local deps to canonical root policy (/data/projects equivalents). Include policy decisions for out-of-root dependencies and clear fallback rationale.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Planner computes closure limited to approved roots under canonical topology rules.
- Planner output contains deterministic sync order, reason metadata, and risk classification for each root.
- Unsafe or unverifiable closure states are explicitly marked for fail-open fallback.
- Planner API is consumable by transfer and preflight stages without additional implicit assumptions.
- Integration tests validate closure plans for single-repo, multi-repo, and intentionally broken graphs.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:03.027337835Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T19:37:18.898470016Z","closed_at":"2026-02-16T19:37:18.898441713Z","close_reason":"Completed dependency-closure planner with deterministic sync ordering, per-root reason/risk metadata, explicit fail-open states, and integration tests; see latest artifact comment","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cargo","path-deps","planner","rch-rel-2026q1"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:12.202400670Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":212,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed `bd-vvmd.2.2` by adding a shared dependency-closure planner layer on top of resolver output, with deterministic sync planning, root-level reason/risk metadata, explicit fail-open states, and integration coverage for single/multi/broken scenarios.

## Summary
Added planner module:
- `rch-common/src/dependency_closure_planner.rs`

Added integration tests:
- `rch-common/tests/dependency_closure_planner.rs`

Exported planner API from shared crate surface:
- `rch-common/src/lib.rs`

## Acceptance Criteria Mapping

### 1) Planner computes closure limited to approved roots under canonical topology rules
Planner resolves through canonical-policy-aware resolver and converts to plan:
- `build_dependency_closure_plan_with_policy` (`rch-common/src/dependency_closure_planner.rs:112`)
- consumes `resolve_cargo_path_dependency_graph_with_policy(...)`, so canonical-root constraints are inherited from resolver policy enforcement.
- resolver failures are converted into explicit fail-open planner states (not silently ignored).

### 2) Output includes deterministic sync order, reason metadata, risk classification per root
Planner output model:
- state/risk/reason enums (`rch-common/src/dependency_closure_planner.rs:20`, `rch-common/src/dependency_closure_planner.rs:28`, `rch-common/src/dependency_closure_planner.rs:38`)
- per-root action struct with metadata and risk (`rch-common/src/dependency_closure_planner.rs:57`)
- final plan struct with ordered actions (`rch-common/src/dependency_closure_planner.rs:77`)

Deterministic ordering logic:
- dependency-first deterministic topological order with lexical tie-breaking via ordered sets/maps (`dependency_first_topological_order`, `rch-common/src/dependency_closure_planner.rs:276`)

Reason/risk assignment:
- `plan_dependency_closure_from_graph` computes per-root reason and risk classes (`rch-common/src/dependency_closure_planner.rs:123`)

### 3) Unsafe/unverifiable closure states explicitly marked for fail-open fallback
Explicit fail-open conversion from resolver/planner unsafe states:
- resolver error -> fail-open plan (`fail_open_plan_from_resolver_error`, `rch-common/src/dependency_closure_planner.rs:340`)
- structured planner issue mapping (`issue_from_resolver_error`, `rch-common/src/dependency_closure_planner.rs:366`)
- non-deterministic planner ordering fallback also emits `FailOpen` state and critical issue (`rch-common/src/dependency_closure_planner.rs:133` block)

### 4) Planner API consumable by transfer/preflight without implicit assumptions
Shared, explicit API in `rch-common`:
- `build_dependency_closure_plan`
- `build_dependency_closure_plan_with_policy`
- `plan_dependency_closure_from_graph`
with data-only return type `DependencyClosurePlan` (`rch-common/src/dependency_closure_planner.rs:77`) that exposes:
- ordered canonical roots (`canonical_roots`)
- full `sync_order` action list
- `fail_open` and `fail_open_reason`
- structured issue list for diagnostics/fallback routing.

Public exports wired in `rch-common/src/lib.rs` so transfer/preflight can consume directly without private-module coupling.

### 5) Integration tests for single-repo, multi-repo, and intentionally broken graphs
Integration coverage added in `rch-common/tests/dependency_closure_planner.rs`:
- single repo ready-plan scenario (`planner_single_repo_generates_ready_plan`, line 74)
- multi-repo deterministic dependency-first order (`planner_multi_repo_produces_deterministic_dependency_first_order`, line 93)
- intentionally broken fixtures assert explicit fail-open + issue metadata (`planner_broken_graphs_mark_fail_open_with_issues`, line 139)

## Validation Evidence
Executed:
1. `cargo fmt --check` -> PASS
2. `cargo test -p rch-common --test dependency_closure_planner -- --nocapture` -> PASS (3/3)
3. `cargo test -p rch-common cargo_path_deps -- --nocapture` -> PASS (8/8)
4. `cargo clippy -p rch-common --all-targets -- -D warnings` -> PASS
5. `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets` -> PASS (remote toolchain missing -> expected local fallback)
6. `rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` -> FAIL on pre-existing unrelated lint:
   - `rch/src/commands/daemon.rs:627` `clippy::items_after_test_module`

Conclusion: `bd-vvmd.2.2` acceptance criteria are satisfied with deterministic planning output, explicit risk/fallback signaling, and integration test coverage across ready and broken closure scenarios.
","created_at":"2026-02-16T19:37:15Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-vvmd.2.3","title":"Extend transfer pipeline to multi-root sync with deterministic manifests","description":"Upgrade transfer.rs flow to sync not only project root but full required repo closure, using deterministic transfer manifests to minimize drift and aid debugging. Ensure exclusions and performance budgets remain enforced. Acceptance: reproducible sync plan and auditable transfer logs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Transfer pipeline supports multi-root rsync/zstd sync from deterministic manifest input.
- Manifest includes content fingerprints and canonical path mapping for each transferred root.
- Pipeline prevents accidental inclusion of disallowed paths/artifacts outside planned closure.
- Partial transfer failures emit structured diagnostics and trigger safe fail-open behavior.
- Integration tests verify successful sync, partial failure recovery, and manifest reproducibility.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:03.235056500Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T01:11:47.674635240Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["path-deps","rch-rel-2026q1","rsync","transfer"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:12.398465795Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":218,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update: wired multi-root runtime sync end-to-end in hook/transfer path and added deterministic dependency-root ordering + verbose traceability. Added custom artifact sync for forwarded CARGO_TARGET_DIR by mirroring worker .rch-target into caller target dir, with regression test hook::tests::test_execute_remote_compilation_syncs_custom_cargo_target_dir_artifacts. Validation: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --workspace --all-targets; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace --all-targets; tests/e2e/path_dependency_fixtures.sh; tests/e2e/repo_updater_contract.sh.","created_at":"2026-02-17T01:11:47Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-vvmd.2.3","title":"Extend transfer pipeline to multi-root sync with deterministic manifests","description":"Upgrade transfer.rs flow to sync not only project root but full required repo closure, using deterministic transfer manifests to minimize drift and aid debugging. Ensure exclusions and performance budgets remain enforced. Acceptance: reproducible sync plan and auditable transfer logs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Transfer pipeline supports multi-root rsync/zstd sync from deterministic manifest input.
- Manifest includes content fingerprints and canonical path mapping for each transferred root.
- Pipeline prevents accidental inclusion of disallowed paths/artifacts outside planned closure.
- Partial transfer failures emit structured diagnostics and trigger safe fail-open behavior.
- Integration tests verify successful sync, partial failure recovery, and manifest reproducibility.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:03.235056500Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T19:21:50.345181784Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["path-deps","rch-rel-2026q1","rsync","transfer"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:12.398465795Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":218,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update: wired multi-root runtime sync end-to-end in hook/transfer path and added deterministic dependency-root ordering + verbose traceability. Added custom artifact sync for forwarded CARGO_TARGET_DIR by mirroring worker .rch-target into caller target dir, with regression test hook::tests::test_execute_remote_compilation_syncs_custom_cargo_target_dir_artifacts. Validation: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --workspace --all-targets; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace --all-targets; tests/e2e/path_dependency_fixtures.sh; tests/e2e/repo_updater_contract.sh.","created_at":"2026-02-17T01:11:47Z"},{"id":225,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update (2026-02-17): cleared active merge-conflict state that was blocking validation (markers removed from install.sh, rch-common/src/patterns.rs, rch/src/fleet/{executor.rs,mod.rs}, rch/src/hook.rs, rch/src/transfer.rs) while preserving upstream transfer/hook behavior. Validation all run via rch offload: rch exec -- cargo fmt --check (ok), rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets (ok), rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings (ok), rch exec -- cargo test -p rch-common --all-targets (1303 passed, 0 failed, 8 ignored).","created_at":"2026-02-17T19:21:50Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.2.4","title":"Add remote pre-build verification of required dependency roots/files","description":"Before remote execution, verify worker has all required dependency roots and critical files for the computed closure. Missing prerequisites should trigger controlled fallback to local execution with precise reason codes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Remote pre-build verifier confirms required dependency roots/files exist and satisfy freshness policy.
- Verification runs before remote execution and blocks unsafe remote attempt paths deterministically.
- Failure modes map to explicit reason codes and remediation guidance in machine-readable output.
- Verifier results are logged with per-root evidence (present/missing/stale/unknown).
- E2E tests validate verified-success path and multiple missing/stale dependency fallback paths.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:03.442824347Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T17:56:31.783447091Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["path-deps","preflight","rch-rel-2026q1","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:12.795231686Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:12.597458402Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:56:22.159168991Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":205,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Dependency update: added  because pre-build required-file verification should run against the deterministic multi-root sync manifest and post-sync remote state produced by . This prevents duplicate manifest logic and reduces divergence risk between sync and verify phases.","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:56:27Z"},{"id":206,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Correction: Dependency update rationale is that bd-vvmd.2.4 now depends on bd-vvmd.2.3 so remote required-file verification runs against the deterministic multi-root manifest and post-sync remote state produced by bd-vvmd.2.3. This avoids duplicate manifest logic and sync/verify divergence.","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:56:31Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.2.5","title":"Update cache/project hashing to include dependency-closure fingerprints","description":"Revise cache key identity so external local dependency states influence project hash/identity. Prevent stale remote cache reuse when dependent repos change. Acceptance: hash changes when any closure member revision/content changes according to policy.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Cache identity includes stable fingerprints for all closure members influencing build outputs.
- Hashing strategy avoids false cache hits when dependent repos change out of band.
- Hash computation remains deterministic across equivalent path alias forms (`/dp` and `/data/projects`).
- Performance impact of expanded hash input is measured and within defined budget.
- Unit tests cover fingerprint changes, unchanged-noise cases, and alias normalization equivalence.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Dicklesworthstone","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:03.646442695Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T01:30:04.607326720Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cache","correctness","path-deps","rch-rel-2026q1"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.5","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:12.994535145Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":221,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update: implemented closure-aware hash computation in transfer layer via compute_project_hash_with_dependency_roots(project_path, dependency_roots), including canonical/alias normalization and key-file content fingerprints for each closure root. Wired hook primary pipeline hash to dependency closure plan roots so dependency-repo manifest changes influence primary project identity. Added regression tests: dependency manifest change invalidates hash; non-key noise file does not perturb hash; canonical vs symlink-alias dependency roots produce identical hash. Validation: cargo fmt; cargo check --workspace --all-targets; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test -p rch compute_project_hash_with_dependency_roots -- --nocapture; cargo test -p rch --all-targets.","created_at":"2026-02-17T01:30:04Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.2.6","title":"Define fail-open semantics for unresolved path dependencies","description":"Specify and implement precise decision matrix: when to attempt remote build, when to trigger convergence/sync, and when to fallback locally. Must avoid silent partial sync behavior that can yield misleading build outcomes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Decision matrix explicitly defines when RCH must fail-open to local execution for unresolved path-dep states.
- Matrix covers timeout, policy-violation, unknown-state, and verifier-failure scenarios.
- Each decision path emits stable reason codes and operator remediation hints.
- Semantics guarantee no unsafe remote execution attempt when prerequisite confidence is insufficient.
- Unit/integration tests validate every matrix branch with deterministic assertions.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:03.849405866Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T16:44:46.771885106Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fail-open","path-deps","policy","rch-rel-2026q1"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:13.194937422Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:13.394997678Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.2.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:13.594874089Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
@@ -418,7 +418,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.4.6","title":"Add unit/integration/E2E tests for disk-full prevention and recovery","description":"Build comprehensive unit/integration/E2E tests for disk-pressure prevention and recovery using shared harness tooling plus targeted unit fixtures. Cover imminent-full, full, post-recovery, and active-build contention states.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Unit tests validate pressure classification and reclaim policy decisions.
- Integration tests validate scheduler admission behavior under pressure transitions.
- E2E tests validate recovery paths and routing away from risky workers before no-space failures.
- Failure-path logs include pressure class, reclaim decisions, scheduling outcomes, and fallback reasons.
- Tests prove no destructive broad cleanup behavior is used in recovery flows.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:06.763125660Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T09:03:45.208056383Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T09:03:45.208025265Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","rch-rel-2026q1","storage","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.4.6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:19.810888070Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.4.6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.4.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:20.019238678Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.4.6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.4.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:15.286184518Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.4.6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:33:13.739381980Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.4.7","title":"Add predictive disk headroom estimator + reservation model","description":"Implement predictive disk headroom model and per-build space reservation estimates so scheduler avoids workers likely to run out of space mid-build. Use closure/planned artifact signals where available and conservative fallback heuristics otherwise.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Headroom estimator computes minimum required free space with confidence bands.
- Scheduler admission logic uses estimator output to reject/deprioritize risky workers before execution.
- Reservation accounting avoids over-committing shared worker disks under concurrency.
- Detailed logs include estimated requirement, observed free space, confidence, and decision path.
- Unit/integration tests validate estimator behavior across small/large builds and concurrent workloads.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:39:45.665066966Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T08:20:58.695617705Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T08:20:58.695588992Z","close_reason":"Implemented predictive disk headroom estimator with reservation model in rchd/src/headroom.rs. HeadroomEstimator uses historical bytes_transferred data to predict build space needs with confidence bands (min/expected/max). Includes concurrent reservation tracking, headroom scoring (0.0-1.0) for worker selection, and fallback estimates when no history available. 21 tests pass, all quality gates clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["performance","rch-rel-2026q1","scheduling","storage","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.4.7","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:14.521905011Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.4.7","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:14.312802517Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":163,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.4.7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"User-outcome rationale: disk-full incidents are best prevented before scheduling. Predictive headroom estimates reduce mid-build failures and avoid wasted remote cycles.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:37Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.5","title":"Workstream: Stuck-Process Detection/Remediation via process_triage","description":"Purpose: address remote worker degradation caused by hung or orphaned compilation processes. Integrate `/data/projects/process_triage` to identify pathological process states, execute bounded remediation policies, and feed health signals back into worker selection so unstable nodes are deprioritized or quarantined.

## Success Criteria
- Detector/remediator pipeline achieves high precision with bounded, auditable actions.
- Periodic and on-demand triage loops are operational and exposed via status surfaces.
- Unresolved process debt influences worker scoring with stable recovery hysteresis.
- Process-triage tests verify safety boundaries, low false-positive behavior, and recovery outcomes.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:01.024580281Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:16.366160512Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["processes","rch","rch-rel-2026q1","stability","workers"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:22:26.054257607Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:24:22.503601592Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:24:22.702430032Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:16.366021632Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":134,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Safety principle: process remediation must be bounded and auditable. No broad kill patterns; only targeted actions derived from correlated build lifecycle evidence. Worker selection penalties reduce repeat incidents while preserving throughput.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:20:21Z"},{"id":145,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Process triage philosophy: prioritize precision over aggressiveness. Detection must correlate process state with known build lifecycle/ownership to avoid false positives. Remediation ladder is bounded (TERM then KILL where allowed), fully logged, and policy-gated. Remaining process debt feeds worker health penalties so degraded workers are deprioritized until stabilized.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:28:09Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-vvmd.5.1","title":"Define process_triage integration contract and safe-action policy","description":"Specify how rchd invokes process_triage and interprets findings. Define strict allowlist/denylist and escalation ladder to avoid terminating legitimate workloads. Include rollback and observability requirements.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Contract specifies request/response schema, timeout/retry policy, and supported triage action classes.
- Safe-action policy defines explicit allowlist, denylist, and escalation thresholds.
- Contract includes audit requirements for every invoked triage/remediation action.
- Failure taxonomy distinguishes detector uncertainty from executor/runtime failures.
- Test plan covers contract conformance and policy enforcement edge cases.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:06.959266657Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T02:02:53.499225676Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["contract","process_triage","rch-rel-2026q1","safety"],"comments":[{"id":216,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Paused to execute current bv --robot-next top pick bd-vvmd.3.1; partial contract draft kept locally for later continuation.","created_at":"2026-02-16T21:05:55Z"},{"id":223,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update: process_triage contract code in rch-common was previously not in the crate module graph (rch_common::e2e::mod did not declare/re-export process_triage), so contract/tests were effectively dormant. Wired module into rch-common e2e public surface ( + explicit re-exports), then fixed schema test resilience for schemars layout variants and resolved a clippy  finding in allow/deny conflict detection. Validation: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --workspace --all-targets; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test -p rch-common process_triage_contract_ -- --nocapture.","created_at":"2026-02-17T02:02:46Z"},{"id":224,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Correction to previous comment (shell quoting artifact removed): process_triage contract code in rch-common was previously outside the active crate module graph because rch-common/src/e2e/mod.rs did not declare or re-export the process_triage module. Wired it into the public e2e surface via module declaration and explicit re-exports, then fixed schema-test robustness for schemars definition layouts and replaced the allow/deny overlap check with a clippy-compliant first-intersection lookup. Validation: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --workspace --all-targets; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test -p rch-common process_triage_contract_ -- --nocapture.","created_at":"2026-02-17T02:02:53Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-vvmd.5.2","title":"Implement stuck-process detector correlated with build lifecycle state","description":"Detect candidate stuck/orphan processes by correlating remote process state, timeouts, slot ownership, and build metadata. Goal: high precision detection with minimal false positives.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Detector correlates process state, build ownership, slot allocation, and heartbeat/progress evidence.
- Confidence scoring differentiates slow-but-healthy jobs from likely orphan/hung processes.
- Detection output includes explainable evidence fields for operator review.
- Detector latency stays within configured triage budget under concurrent workloads.
- Unit/integration tests cover jitter, transient telemetry gaps, and false-positive suppression.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:07.166793804Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T16:45:43.657327122Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["daemon","detection","process_triage","rch-rel-2026q1"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:16.608936776Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:15.940719864Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":151,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Detection quality bar: never classify by runtime alone. Correlate PID trees with build ownership, slot lifecycle, command fingerprints, and timeout envelopes. Model should support confidence levels so remediation policy can require higher confidence for destructive actions.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:28:31Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.5.3","title":"Implement bounded remediation pipeline (TERM/KILL escalation with auditing)","description":"Add remediation executor with conservative escalation, bounded retries, and full audit logging. Must never apply broad destructive process actions and must preserve system safety invariants.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Remediation pipeline enforces bounded escalation (observe -> TERM -> KILL) with explicit guardrails.
- Actions are scoped to verified offender processes only; no broad kill patterns are permitted.
- Every action emits audit record with evidence, rationale, actor, and outcome.
- Pipeline supports abort/rollback behavior when confidence drops or safety checks fail.
- Integration tests validate escalation correctness, safety boundaries, and audit completeness.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:07.370040536Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T17:08:14.349135597Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["process_triage","rch-rel-2026q1","remediation","safety"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:07:29.723547694Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:16.809138818Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":191,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Dependency tightened: remediation executor now explicitly depends on integration safety contract (bd-vvmd.5.1) to prevent implementation drift between policy and action pipeline.","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:08:14Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.5.4","title":"Add periodic triage loop + on-demand operator triage command","description":"Provide scheduled triage and manual trigger paths, with configurable cadence and time budgets. Expose triage outcomes in machine-readable status output and human remediation hints.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Periodic loop cadence is configurable and does not starve active scheduling paths.
- On-demand command supports scoped triage runs with clear machine/human output modes.
- Loop and command share deterministic decision pipeline to avoid divergent behavior.
- Time-budget exhaustion is surfaced explicitly with partial-result semantics.
- Tests verify scheduling cadence, manual trigger behavior, and output determinism.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:07.576921402Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T16:45:44.189818174Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["maintenance","operations","process_triage","rch-rel-2026q1"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.4","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:17.006389954Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
